findings.description: Tribe index mismatch: SelectScene sorts tribes alphabetically by name and passes the sorted index as humanTribeIndex. GameScene uses the unsorted TRIBE_CONFIGS array to resolve the index. Every tribe selection starts the wrong game. Confirmed: clicking Elyrion starts Bardur (Elyrion needs sorted idx 2 → maps to unsorted idx 2 = Bardur). Full mapping: Bardur→Xin-xi, Cymanti→Imperius, Elyrion→Bardur, Imperius→Oumaji, Oumaji→Polaris, Polaris→Cymanti, Xin-xi→Elyrion.
findings.evidence: Live verification at hex.codeovertcp.com. GameScene shows humanTribe={id:'bardur', name:'Bardur', techs:['HUNTING']} after clicking Elyrion card. Source: SelectScene.ts line 109 sorts TRIBE_CONFIGS, GameScene.ts line 115 uses unsorted TRIBE_CONFIGS.

findings.description: AI territory expansion and AI difficulty differentiation spec_gaps stagnant for 5+ cycles (cycles_stagnant=5). Previously escalated but no fix committed. Hard AI is not observably more effective than Easy AI.
findings.evidence: Checkpoint .checkpoint.json spec_gaps entries, both at cycles_stagnant=5, status='escalated'

tasks_generated.description: Fix tribe index mismatch between SelectScene and GameScene. SelectScene sorts TRIBE_CONFIGS alphabetically by name (line 109) and passes the sorted index as humanTribeIndex. GameScene uses the unsorted TRIBE_CONFIGS array (line 115) to resolve the index. Fix: pass the tribe id or name instead of the sorted index, OR sort TRIBE_CONFIGS in GameScene the same way, OR pass the index into the original (unsorted) array.
tasks_generated.success_criteria: Clicking each tribe card starts the correct tribe. Elyrion starts with Ecology tech and Elyrion special units. Bardur starts with Hunting tech. Xin-xi starts with Riding tech. All 7 tribe cards map to the correct game.
tasks_generated.coach_checks: Open hex.codeovertcp.com, click each of the 7 tribes, verify the started game shows the correct tribe name and starting tech via HUD.

tasks_generated.description: Implement strategic territory expansion for AI — prioritize high-value resource tiles (fruits, animals, minerals) and strategic chokepoints instead of basic wander-to-unseen behavior. Part of fix-ai-opponent-intelligence item 3.
tasks_generated.success_criteria: AI units visibly move toward resource-rich tiles and claim valuable territory. AI cities grow faster when resources are nearby.
tasks_generated.coach_checks: Start a game vs AI, observe AI movement patterns over 30+ turns. Verify AI claims fruit/animal tiles near its cities.

tasks_generated.description: Implement observable strategic differences between Easy/Medium/Hard AI beyond basic options config. Hard AI should win more, have higher scores, and make smarter tactical decisions. Add difficulty-specific decision weights.
tasks_generated.success_criteria: Hard AI observably captures more cities and achieves higher scores than Easy AI across multiple games. Clear behavioral difference visible.
tasks_generated.coach_checks: Play 3 games vs Easy and 3 vs Hard. Compare AI city count, score, and tactical decisions at turn 30.
